summary: Frontend-only regression for 999 Taxi landing after Cobalt GLB re-optimization + material/decal fix. 3D visual integrity is EXCELLENT: crisp glossy yellow sedan silhouette, chrome/silver alloy wheels (no more white blobs), realistic transparent headlights, continuous checker band + aligned '999 TAXI' door decal, orange roof taxi sign. GLB /models/cobalt.glb?v=3 returns 200. Hero stages 0/1/2/3 all render with sticky canvas, no blank frames. Stage 3 CTAs correct (t.me/t_boxodir, tel:+998781139991). Desktop AND mobile: zero console errors, zero runtime overlay. Mobile 390x844: no horizontal overflow, sticky bottom CTA bar present, bordered canvas below CTAs renders car. All sections present (marquee, about, services, driver-advantages, testimonials, office-location, faq, leadform, final-cta, footer). Footer 3D credit 'Q.SARDOR / CC BY 4.0' present.
critical_code_review_comments: Leadform inputs lack name attributes and data-testid; adding data-testid='leadform-name', 'leadform-phone', 'leadform-submit' would make e2e tests deterministic (currently must locate by placeholder).
FAQ accordion buttons lack stable data-testid — added would help regression suites.

Lenis smooth scroll causes Playwright scroll_into_view_if_needed to time out on off-screen elements; consider exposing a `window.__lenis?.scrollTo` helper or briefly disabling Lenis in a test-mode flag.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Pure frontend app (no backend). GLB re-optimization (v=3, 1.33MB draco, full geometry preserved) fully resolves the previous 'broken car' bug. First-visit 3D load ~8-12s. Use animations='disabled' + timeout=60000 for stage-3 screenshots. For lead form regression, locate inputs by placeholder ('Ismingiz', '+998', '@username') because inputs have no name/data-testid. Suggest main agent add data-testid attributes to lead form + FAQ for future automation reliability.
